tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The lion is one of the largest big cats and is often called the king of the jungle. Lions live in groups known as prides and are known for their strength and majestic appearance.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: Male lions have large manes that make them appear bigger and protect them during fights. Lions are powerful hunters and work together in groups to catch prey.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Lions are the only cats that live in social groups. Their roar can be heard up to 8 kilometers away.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Elephants are the largest land animals on Earth and are known for their long trunks and large ears.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: Elephants use their trunks for breathing, smelling, grabbing objects and making sounds. They are highly intelligent animals with strong family bonds.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Elephants have excellent memory and can recognize individuals even after many years.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Dolphins are highly intelligent marine mammals known for their playful behavior and strong social bonds.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: They communicate with whistles and clicks and use echolocation to locate objects and prey underwater.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Dolphins are considered among the smartest animals in the world.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Bats are the only mammals capable of true flight. They play an important role in ecosystems by controlling insect populations and pollinating plants.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: Bats use echolocation to navigate and hunt in complete darkness.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: There are more than 1,400 species of bats making them one of the largest groups of mammals.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Kangaroos are large marsupials native to Australia and are famous for their powerful hind legs and jumping ability.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: They carry their young in a pouch called a marsupium and can travel long distances with powerful jumps.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Kangaroos can jump up to 9 meters in a single leap.
